title: invoice_statement orientation detection improvement

rootCause.description: detect_orientation used hardcoded target_short=224px thumbnail. At 224px, small Korean/numeric text in invoice_statement is insufficient to differentiate 0deg vs 180deg scores, causing angle=0 return on upside-down documents.

changes: Added target_short: int = 224 parameter (default unchanged for backward compat)
skip_second_pass=True forces early stop after first_pass (0/180 only)
Added target_short to return meta dict
templateImageNormalization meta: added orientationTargetShort, orientationMode fields

performanceExpectation.note: 512px 2-pass vs 224px 2-4-pass. Total pixel count comparable. Should not regress to T-27 levels.

nextSteps: Live RunOCR: normal 1.jpg → verify appliedRotation=0, tableRows=28, totalAmount correct
Live RunOCR: flipped 1.jpg → verify appliedRotation=180, correct parse
Run All: invoice_statement 7 samples → rowCount exact 7/7
Monitor processing time: should remain ~110s not exceed 153s
Verify baseline_fast / google results unchanged (non-template path)
T-28c (if needed): handle 90/270 rotated invoice_statement separately
